critique.summary: The article announces the release of FFmpeg 8.1 "Hoare," detailing new features such as experimental xHE-AAC decoding, Vulkan compute-based codecs improvements, and various hardware encoding supports. It also summarizes previous major releases, highlighting advancements in codec support, hardware acceleration, and internal improvements over time.
mainPoints: FFmpeg 8.1 "Hoare" introduces experimental xHE-AAC Mps212 MPEG-H decoding and Vulkan compute-based codec enhancements.
The release includes support for new decoders, filters, and hardware encoding capabilities such as D3D12 and Rockchip H.264/HEVC.
Previous major releases like FFmpeg 8.0 "Huffman" and 7.1 "Péter" added native decoders, Vulkan-based codecs, and improved hardware acceleration.
FFmpeg has progressively modernized its infrastructure, added multi-threaded processing, and improved codec and filter performance.
The project is supported by sponsors like Germany's Sovereign Tech Fund and participates in initiatives like Google Summer of Code.
critique.truthfulness: The information in the article is consistent with known FFmpeg release practices and features documented on the official FFmpeg website and related technical blogs. The detailed changelogs, feature lists, and dates align with FFmpeg's public release history and development updates. The mention of Vulkan compute-based codecs, hardware acceleration, and new codec support is corroborated by official FFmpeg announcements and technical documentation. The sponsorship and community engagement details are also verifiable through external sources. Overall, the article presents accurate and truthful information about FFmpeg releases and developments.
considerations: The article does not provide independent third-party evaluations or user feedback on the new features and performance improvements.
Potential limitations or known issues with the new experimental features, such as xHE-AAC decoding, are not discussed.
The impact of these updates on various operating systems or hardware configurations is not detailed.
Security implications or vulnerabilities addressed in these releases are not mentioned.
The article assumes familiarity with technical terms and FFmpeg's development context, which may limit accessibility for general audiences.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around the new FFmpeg 8.1 release, with users sharing their appreciation for the tool, noting new features, and discussing usability and hardware support. Some comments highlight technical details and concerns about hardware encoding support, while others express wishes for simpler usage.

comments.author: gyan
comments.text:
Changelog:

ffprobe -codec option

EXIF Metadata Parsing

gfxcapture: Windows.Graphics.Capture based window/monitor capture

hxvs demuxer for HXVS/HXVT IP camera format

MPEG-H 3D Audio decoding via mpeghdec

D3D12 H.264 encoder

drawvg filter via libcairo

ffmpeg CLI tiled HEIF support

D3D12 AV1 encoder

ProRes Vulkan hwaccel

DPX Vulkan hwaccel

Rockchip H.264/HEVC hardware encoder

Add vf_scale_d3d12 filter

JPEG-XS parser

JPEG-XS decoder and encoder through libsvtjpegxs

JPEG-XS raw bitstream muxer and demuxer

IAMF Projection mode Ambisonic Audio Elements muxing and demuxing

Add vf_mestimate_d3d12 filter

xHE-AAC Mps212 decoding support (experimental)

Remove the old HLS protocol handler

Vulkan compute codec optimizations

swscale Vulkan support

LCEVC metadata bitstream filter

Add vf_deinterlace_d3d12 filter

ffprobe: only show refs field in stream section when reading frames

ProRes Vulkan encoder

LCEVC parser

LCEVC enhancement layer exporting in MPEG-TS
comments.analysis: This comment provides a detailed and accurate changelog of the FFmpeg 8.1 release, reflecting the official update information.

comments.author: edgarvaldes
comments.text: One of the best open-source tools out there. I'm a frequent user of Plex, Jellyfin, Tunarr, local music files, etc. I use it weekly to extract subtitles, trim videos, convert music formats, and remove audio tracks. After writing the previous paragraph, I realized I've never donated to the project; it's time to change that.
comments.analysis: The comment shares a personal positive experience with FFmpeg, which aligns with the tool's known versatility and widespread use.

comments.author: shevy-java
comments.text:
FFmpeg is really great. The only wish I'd have is for the usage to become simpler - both for regular stuff, but also for advanced filtering.

If anyone remembers, avisynth was pretty cool back in the days. You could kind of script video/audio manipulations, a bit like a UNIX/Linux pipe, but kind of simpler, in my opinion. FFmpeg allows for many similar operations, but remembering anything here is ... hard. I'd love for the whole usage API to become much simpler, but it seems nobody among the ffmpeg dev team is considering this. :(

I can't be the only one with that wish though ...

It does not diminish ffmpeg being so great in general, but I think it could be better.
comments.analysis: This comment offers a constructive critique on FFmpeg's usability, a common sentiment among users due to its complexity.

comments.author: megous
comments.text: I wouldn't get too excited about rockchip hw encoding. It's rkmpp based, not an upstream solution. You'd need Rockchip kernel for this, I guess.
comments.analysis: Provides a realistic caution about the hardware encoding support, highlighting dependency on specific kernels which limits general usability.

comments.author: pandaforce
comments.text: Khronos published a post on the Vulkan compute codecs in FFmpeg: https://www.khronos.org/blog/video-encoding-and-decoding-wit...
comments.analysis: Shares a relevant external resource about Vulkan compute codecs in FFmpeg, adding value to the discussion.

comments.author: brcmthrowaway
comments.text: How much of this release was done by to corporate/big tech employees?
comments.analysis: Raises a question about contributor demographics but does not provide information or evidence.

comments.text:
> Oh there's a new version of ffmpeg, I'll just quickly build it from source... no I can't wait I'll download the binary

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9kaIXkImCAM
comments.analysis: This comment uses humor and a video link to express impatience, but it adds no factual content to the discussion.

comments.author: junelee77
comments.analysis: A brief compliment that does not contribute meaningful information or analysis.
